description_long: This object is a stylized mushroom with a vibrant red cap adorned with several white, irregularly spaced spots. The cap is polygonal and conical, tapering to a point at the top and flaring outward with a straight margin. The stem is elongated, cylindrical, and slightly bent, transitioning from a dark brown upper section to a lighter, grayish lower section. The surface of the cap appears matte and smooth, while the stem has a faceted, low-poly look. No gills are visible beneath the cap, and the mushroom stands upright, suggesting it is intended as a decorative or environmental element. The overall appearance is reminiscent of the iconic Amanita muscaria, often found in parks or urban green spaces.
description: A stylized red mushroom with white spots, a straight-edged cap, and a bent, two-toned stem. The cap is smooth and matte, while the stem is faceted and cylindrical.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red cap with white spots and the straight, upright stem.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the bent, faceted stem and the underside of the cap.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full length of the stem and the cap's polygonal shape.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the curvature of the stem and the distribution of white spots on the cap.
